channel.title: Pages on Marks2Games
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on Marks2Games

item.title: About
item.description:
About Marks2Games

We are two game enthusiasts for whom games have always been an inseparable part of life.

Our friendship began with the now-defunct online strategy game Red Dragon, and since then we have spent years exploring and playing many different game titles together. We enjoy watching how the world of games constantly evolves, and we are always excited to discover new mechanics, systems, and design ideas. Since we are not short of ideas ourselves, we decided to contribute with creations of our own.

Each of our games begins with a core mechanic that gradually shapes the overall theme, while other supporting elements are added along the way. Our goal is to create innovative and fresh game experiences — or at the very least, unusual combinations of existing mechanics.

At the same time, we want to make games that avoid the pay-to-win model and let players play on their own terms, without unnecessary pressure. We are players who do not want to be controlled by games, but rather enjoy them in the moments we choose.

The Team

Marek Basovník

Since childhood, I have been fascinated by puzzles, although I still cannot solve a Rubik’s Cube — partly because I keep challenging myself to learn without a guide, and without even having the cube at hand.

As a young boy, I was drawn to mathematics, and later I studied theoretical physics at the Faculty of Mathematics and Physics, where I explored the horizons of black holes. Through theoretical physics, I came to realize that understanding the real world makes it easier to navigate imaginary ones as well, including the worlds of games.

If you want to move from being a user to being a creator, the key lies in understanding principles much deeper than they may first appear.

Marek Váchal

My journey into fantasy and games began when I was six years old and discovered the magic of Dungeons & Dragons with my father. Since then, tabletop RPGs and board games have remained a part of my life, and I continue to play them to this day. Computer games, especially simulation and strategy games, also became one of my main hobbies.

As a teenager, I encountered Indian philosophy, which immediately captivated me and strongly influenced my intellectual path. After finishing high school in bookselling, I continued my studies at the Faculty of Arts, where I specialized in Indian traditions at the Department of Religious Studies.

Ironically, studying this distant culture led me back toward a deeper exploration of Western culture. Along the way, I came to a profound realization: the world of fantasy today is still shaped primarily by Western imagination, even though fantasy itself can be far more imaginative than most people ever expect.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Contact

If you would like to get in touch with Marks2Games, feel free to contact us through the channels below.

We welcome feedback, questions, collaboration ideas, and community messages.

General Inquiries

For general questions about our games, development, or future plans, please contact us at:

Email: your-email@example.com

Community

You can also follow or contact us through our community channels:

  • Discord: https://discord.gg/H4hFa3vgvZ
  • Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/979557247091776/

Partnerships and Media

If you are interested in collaboration, press inquiries, or other partnership opportunities, please use the email above and mention the purpose of your message in the subject line.

Feedback

We are always interested in hearing from players, readers, and supporters. Whether you want to share feedback, report a problem, or simply say hello, we would be glad to hear from you.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: 28 March 2026

This Privacy Policy explains how Marks2Games (“we”, “our”, or “us”) collects, uses, and protects information when you use our website, games, and related services.

At this time, this policy applies primarily to Gravity Game, as it is the currently published digital product under Marks2Games.

1. Who we are

Marks2Games is an independent game project and development brand operated by its creators.

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, you can contact us at:

Email: gravitygame2024@gmail.com

2. Information we may collect

Depending on how you use our services, we may collect the following types of information.

Account and sign-in information

If you sign in to Gravity Game using Google authentication, we may collect:

  • your display name
  • your email address
  • your profile picture

If you use a guest account, an anonymous account identifier may be created instead.

Gameplay and account data

We may collect and store gameplay-related information such as:

  • game progress
  • in-game statistics
  • matchmaking or multiplayer-related data
  • account settings and preferences

This data is used to make the game function properly and to improve the player experience.

Technical and device information

We may also collect non-personal technical information such as:

  • device type
  • operating system version
  • app version
  • performance and crash-related information
  • general usage analytics

This helps us improve stability, compatibility, and overall user experience.

3. How we use your information

We may use collected information for the following purposes:

  • to enable authentication and account access
  • to save and restore game progress
  • to support online and multiplayer functionality
  • to improve gameplay balance and technical performance
  • to diagnose crashes, bugs, and service issues
  • to protect the service against abuse, cheating, or fraudulent activity

We do not use your personal data for advertising sales or data brokerage.

4. Third-party services

Some parts of our services rely on third-party tools and infrastructure providers.

These may include:

  • Firebase (Google LLC) – for authentication, cloud storage, and backend services
  • Google Play Services – for sign-in and Android platform-related services
  • Sentry – for crash reporting, diagnostics, and technical monitoring

These providers may process limited data as necessary for their services to function.

We do not sell your personal data to third parties.

5. Data retention

We keep data only for as long as reasonably necessary to operate and improve our services, maintain account functionality, resolve disputes, prevent abuse, and comply with legal obligations.

Some gameplay or account data may remain stored until:

  • the account is deleted,
  • the service is discontinued,
  • or retention is no longer necessary.

6. Account deletion and data requests

If you wish to delete your account and associated data for Gravity Game, you may do so:

  • through available in-app account settings (if enabled), or
  • by contacting us via email

Contact for deletion requests:
gravitygame2024@gmail.com

We will make reasonable efforts to process deletion requests in an appropriate timeframe.

7. Data security

We take reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your information from unauthorized access, misuse, alteration, or disclosure.

However, no online service can be guaranteed to be completely secure, and use of the service is always at your own risk.

8. Children’s privacy

Our services are not intentionally designed to collect personal data from children without appropriate legal basis or parental involvement where required by law.

If you believe personal data has been submitted inappropriately, please contact us and we will review the situation.

9. Changes to this Privacy Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time.

If significant changes are made, we may communicate them through:

  • the website
  • the app
  • or other reasonable means

The most current version will always be published on this page.

10. Contact

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how your data is handled, please contact us:

Email: gravitygame2024@gmail.com

item.title: Support
item.description:
Support Marks2Games

Marks2Games is an independent studio project built out of enthusiasm for games, worldbuilding, and original mechanics.

If you would like to support our work, you can do so through Buy Me a Coffee:

Buy Me a Coffee: https://buymeacoffee.com/gravitygame?l=en

Your support helps us invest more time and resources into development, writing, design, testing, and future releases.

What Your Support Helps With

By supporting Marks2Games, you help us continue working on:

  • Gravity Game — our sci-fi online strategy game for Android
  • Insect Steampunk — our pen-and-paper RPG project
  • Gladiators — our modern card-based board game in development

Support also helps with general production costs such as art, tools, publishing, playtesting, and long-term development.

Why Support Matters

We want to create games that are imaginative, mechanically interesting, and fair to players. We are especially committed to avoiding pay-to-win design and making games that people can enjoy on their own terms.

Every form of support helps us move a little further.

Thank You

Whether you support us financially, share our work, or simply follow our projects, thank you for helping Marks2Games grow.

item.title: Terms of Service
item.description:
Terms of Service

Effective date: 28 March 2026

These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern your use of the Marks2Games website, games, and related services.

At this time, these Terms apply primarily to Gravity Game, which is the currently published digital product under Marks2Games.

By accessing or using our website, games, or related services, you agree to these Terms.

1. Use of the service

You may use our services only for their intended purpose and in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

You agree not to use our services in ways that could harm the game, other users, or the integrity of the service.

2. Accounts and access

Some features may require account access, whether through Google sign-in, guest access, or future account systems.

You are responsible for:

  • maintaining the security of your device and account access
  • any activity performed through your account
  • using the service in good faith

We may suspend or restrict access in cases of abuse, cheating, malicious behavior, or other misuse of the service.

3. Acceptable use

You agree not to:

  • attempt to hack, exploit, reverse engineer, or disrupt the service
  • use cheats, bots, scripts, or unauthorized automation
  • interfere with multiplayer systems or matchmaking
  • abuse bugs or technical vulnerabilities
  • harass, spam, or intentionally disrupt other users

We reserve the right to take reasonable action in response to misuse, including limiting or terminating access where appropriate.

4. Intellectual property

Unless otherwise stated, the content of the website, games, texts, systems, visuals, and related project materials are the property of Marks2Games or their respective creators.

You may not copy, redistribute, republish, or commercially use project materials without permission, except where explicitly allowed.

This does not prevent normal personal use of the game or lawful sharing such as linking to the public website.

5. Game balance and service changes

We may update, modify, rebalance, restrict, or discontinue parts of our services at any time.

This may include changes to:

  • gameplay systems
  • multiplayer features
  • progression
  • balance
  • availability of online features
  • project documentation or downloadable materials

We are not obligated to maintain all features permanently.

6. Privacy

Your use of the service is also governed by our Privacy Policy.

Please review it to understand how information may be collected and used.

7. No guarantees

Our services are provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis.

We do not guarantee that the service will always be:

  • uninterrupted
  • error-free
  • secure
  • available at all times
  • compatible with every device or system

We will make reasonable efforts to keep the service functional and improve it over time, but we cannot guarantee perfection or permanent availability.

8. Limitation of liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Marks2Games and its creators are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages resulting from the use of the website, game, or related services.

This includes, for example:

  • loss of progress
  • service interruptions
  • technical issues
  • temporary unavailability
  • compatibility problems

Use of the service is at your own discretion and risk.

9. Termination or suspension

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate access to the service if a user violates these Terms or misuses the service in a way that harms the game, its systems, or other users.

Where appropriate, we may also remove access to specific features rather than terminate access entirely.

10. Changes to these Terms

We may update these Terms from time to time.

The current version will always be published on this page. Continued use of the service after updates may be treated as acceptance of the revised Terms.

11. Contact

If you have any questions about these Terms, you can contact us at:

Email: gravitygame2024@gmail.com
